sources.title: R3F Introduction (carried from bench-002)
sources.summary: R3F v9.5.0 for React 19, native reconciler, WebGL2 default

sources.title: COPPA Rule (16 CFR Part 312)
sources.summary: COPPA applies to operators collecting personal info from under-13. Amended 2025, compliance deadline April 22, 2026.

sources.title: COPPA 2025 Amendment (Federal Register)
sources.summary: Major COPPA overhaul: expanded PI definition, data retention limits, enhanced Internal Operations Exception disclosure. Compliance deadline April 22, 2026.

sources.title: Palantir Digital Twin — LEARN Mechanisms
sources.summary: §PHIL.DT-07: LEARN-01 (write-back), LEARN-02 (eval feedback), LEARN-03 (outcome tracking). LEARN stores outcomes as DATA — in this context, regulated children's data.

sources.title: Palantir Security — Four-Layer Model
sources.summary: §SEC.R-02: RBAC (Layer 1) + Markings (Layer 2) + Object-Level (Layer 3) + Policies (Layer 4). Maps to teacher/student role separation.

sources.title: 16 CFR 312.2 — COPPA Personal Information Definition
sources.summary: 11 categories of personal information. Student name (Cat 1) + persistent identifier/login (Cat 7) + scores combined with identifier (Cat 11) = PI.

sources.title: 34 CFR 99.31 — FERPA School Official Exception
sources.summary: EdTech vendor qualifies as school official if: (1) performs institutional service, (2) under direct control of school re: records, (3) subject to redisclosure restrictions. 'Direct control' not precisely defined.

sources.title: Wiley Law — COPPA 2025 Final Rule Analysis
sources.summary: CRITICAL: School consent provisions were proposed in Dec 2023 NPRM but NOT codified in 2025 final rule. FTC deferred to anticipated DOE FERPA updates.

sources.title: Clever — Compliance Documentation
sources.summary: Clever holds COPPA Safe Harbor certification from iKeepSafe. Operates as FERPA school official. Places consent responsibility on school.

sources.title: Google Workspace for Education — Privacy FAQ
sources.summary: Google claims COPPA+FERPA support for Core Services. School provides consent on parents' behalf. But third-party apps using Firebase are NOT covered by Google's compliance.

sources.title: Convex Pricing + Compliance
sources.summary: Free tier: 1M calls/mo, 0.5GB DB. SOC 2 Type II: YES. COPPA: NO explicit certification or documentation. Startup program: up to 1yr free Professional.

sources.title: Firebase COPPA Configuration
sources.summary: Firebase is the ONLY BaaS with explicit COPPA documentation. Developers MUST disable Analytics/Crashlytics/AdID for child-directed apps. Firebase SDK does NOT automatically make app COPPA-compliant.

sources.title: Palantir DevCon 5 — §DC5-05 Four Ontology Design Principles
sources.summary: §DC5-05: Four design principles for ontology (= software for the organization): DDD, DRY (rule of three), OCP (open-closed), PECS. Applicable to state schema design.

sources.title: Palantir DevCon 5 — §DC5-06 Ontology Primitives That Matter
sources.summary: §DC5-06: Primitives — Interfaces (polymorphism), Structs (compound data), Reducers (canonical focus value + history), Derived Properties (business logic near ontology), Object-backed Link Types. Reducers directly model stateful aggregation.

sources.title: Bun Official Docs — File I/O APIs (Bun.file, Bun.write, FileSink)
sources.summary: Bun.file(path) returns lazy BunFile (Blob subtype). Bun.write() writes via optimized syscalls. FileSink API for incremental writes. JSON importable as module or via .json(). All APIs natively TypeScript without transpilation.

sources.title: TypeScript Schema Validation Comparison 2026 — Zod vs Valibot vs TypeBox
sources.summary: TypeBox+Ajv ~8ms, Valibot ~85ms, Zod v4 ~180ms for 100k iterations. Valibot bundle ~1.37KB vs Zod ~17.7KB for simple schemas. TypeBox outputs JSON Schema (OpenAPI-compatible). Zod most ergonomic for simple cases.

claims.text: Student name/ID + scores + session data stored server-side = personal information under COPPA
COPPA 2025 compliance deadline is April 22, 2026 (~1 month from now)
Schools MAY provide COPPA consent on behalf of parents for educational purposes — but exact conditions and limitations are unclear without legal review
FERPA likely applies to EdTech vendors storing student education records — requires data use agreement with school
Developer/operator of EdTech app is the COPPA operator regardless of which BaaS is used — BaaS is processor only
COPPA compliance for EdTech collecting children's data requires AT MINIMUM: privacy policy, consent mechanism, data retention limits, data deletion capability, breach notification plan
Palantir DevCon 5 defines four ontology design principles: DDD (Domain-Driven Design), DRY (rule of three), OCP (Open-Closed), PECS (Producer Extends Consumer Super)
DRY principle in ontology design = 'rule of three' — abstract a pattern only after it has been repeated at least 3 times
Reducers are an ontology primitive that collapse multi-value property histories into a canonical focus value while preserving full history
claims.note: Directly applicable to file-based state management: current state (focus value) + audit log (preserved history)

claims.text: Bun.file(path) returns a BunFile (extends Blob) that is lazily loaded — no disk read occurs until .text(), .json(), .arrayBuffer(), .bytes(), or .stream() is called
Bun.write(path, data) writes to file system using optimized system calls and supports strings, JSON-serialized objects, Blob instances, and HTTP Response bodies
Bun.file(path).writer() returns a FileSink with .write(), .flush(), .end() for incremental/streaming writes — suitable for append-only state logs
TypeBox+Ajv achieves ~8ms for 100,000 iterations on complex object schemas; Valibot ~85ms; Zod v4 ~180ms; Zod v3 ~400ms
claims.note: Benchmark source is tier-3 blog — treat as directional, not definitive

claims.text: Valibot bundle size ~1.37KB vs Zod ~17.7KB for a simple login-form schema — ~90% reduction due to modular tree-shaking architecture
TypeBox generates JSON Schema-compatible output, making it the preferred choice when OpenAPI spec generation or Fastify schema integration is required

evidence.claim: COPPA triggered by student data persistence
evidence.quote: COPPA applies to operators of websites or online services directed to children under 13 that collect personal information
evidence.interpretation: Name/ID + scores + timestamps = personal information. Server-side storage = collection. COPPA is certainly triggered.

evidence.quote: ACTION outcomes are written back as new DATA entities... feedback captured as DATA in the ontology
evidence.interpretation: §PHIL.DT-07: LEARN-01 and LEARN-02 store student outcomes. In EdTech context, this is children's personal information.

evidence.claim: Reducers model state management with canonical current value + history
evidence.quote: Reducers — collapse multi-value property histories into a canonical focus value while preserving history
evidence.interpretation: §DC5-06: Reducer pattern directly maps to file-based state: write current state to state.json, preserve history in append log.

evidence.claim: DRY rule of three prevents premature abstraction in state schema design
evidence.interpretation: §DC5-05: State schema fields should only be abstracted into shared types after appearing in 3+ state documents.

evidenceGaps.description: COPPA school consent mechanism: exact conditions under which schools can act as parental consent agent for EdTech. FTC guidance exists but has not been reviewed by legal counsel for THIS specific scenario.
evidenceGaps.resolvableBy: Legal review by education privacy attorney

evidenceGaps.description: FERPA applicability: whether this specific EdTech vendor scenario requires a FERPA data use agreement. DOE guidance exists but application to a solo developer is unclear.
Convex COPPA posture: whether Convex offers a Data Processing Agreement suitable for children's educational data. Not verified.
Firebase children's privacy: exact scope of Firebase's children's privacy controls and whether they extend to third-party app developers.
District vendor assessment requirements: whether target school districts require SOC 2 or equivalent. Varies by district.
evidenceGaps.resolvableBy: Direct inquiry to target district IT

evidenceGaps.description: Bun FileSink atomicity: whether Bun.file().writer() provides atomic write guarantees or risks partial writes on process termination. Not confirmed in docs reviewed.
evidenceGaps.resolvableBy: Bun GitHub issues / official docs deep read

evidenceGaps.description: TypeBox vs Valibot for CLI-first (no browser) TypeScript: benchmark source is tier-3 blog. No Bun-specific runtime benchmarks found.
evidenceGaps.resolvableBy: Run bun bench locally with representative state schema
